sections.heading: Executive Structural Reading
sections.intent_line: Frame the case in one defensible structural view.
sections.conclusion_line: Conclusion: continuity pressure is manageable only with explicit downside containment thresholds.
sections.support_line: Exposure concentration sits in timing and transition friction rather than role-fit alone.
sections.basis_line: Assessment basis: intake completeness and internal consistency checks.

items: Key implication: commitment requires tighter proof thresholds before acceleration.
Interpretive note: governance burden rises when timing assumptions remain untested.

sections.heading: Structural Risk Diagnosis
sections.conclusion_line: Risk concentration is highest in conversion timing and downside liquidity coverage.
sections.support_line: Risk does not sit evenly across categories; execution exposure is front-loaded.
sections.basis_line: Structural basis: concentration of risk across market and execution nodes.

items: Fragility marker: confidence drops when timeline assumptions are not testable in sequence.

sections.heading: Career Value Structure
sections.intent_line: Clarify what is protected versus surrendered.
sections.conclusion_line: The current path protects continuity value but constrains repositioning value capture.
sections.support_line: Value asymmetry is defined by optionality trade-off, not by headline role appeal.
sections.basis_line: Structural basis: continuity protection versus repositioning demand.

items: Value implication: defer stronger commitments until repositioning proof burden is reduced.

sections.heading: Career Mobility Structure
sections.intent_line: Read transferability, conversion burden, and timing exposure.
sections.conclusion_line: Mobility is feasible but burdened by sequence risk and transition friction.
sections.support_line: Transferability exists, yet conversion burden increases under compressed timing.
sections.basis_line: Structural basis: transferability versus conversion load under time pressure.

items: Mobility implication: preserve optionality while lowering near-term conversion friction.

sections.heading: Strategic Temperament
sections.conclusion_line: Current posture is disciplined but can tighten too slowly when proof windows narrow.
sections.support_line: Governance strength is caution; governance risk is delayed threshold activation.
sections.basis_line: Assessment basis: decision posture under uncertainty and constraint.

items: Temperament implication: convert caution into explicit trigger discipline.

sections.heading: Decision Conditions
sections.conclusion_line: Commitment becomes more defensible once timing, liquidity, and conversion assumptions are jointly verified.
sections.support_line: Threshold clarity matters more than confidence language.
sections.basis_line: Structural basis: commitment defensibility under bounded uncertainty.

items: Condition 2: conversion burden validated against timeline reality.
Condition 3: governance triggers defined before acceleration.
